channel.title: Secrets on Automate everything
channel.description: Recent content in Secrets on Automate everything

item.title: Secrets hide in your Git history, even after you delete them
item.author: sebastian@graef.io (Sebastian Gräf)
item.description: You committed an API key by accident. You notice, delete the line, commit again, and push. Crisis averted, right? No. That key is still sitting in your git history, one git log -p away from anyone who clones the repo. A new commit only adds a layer on top. It never removes the old one.

item.title: The Silent Leak: Why You Should Never Paste Keys into AI Chats
item.author: sebastian@graef.io (Sebastian Gräf)
item.description: You’re debugging a failing API call. Frustrated, you paste your key straight into the chat with GitHub Copilot or Claude to get a faster answer. Feels harmless, right? It’s not. And the problem goes deeper than you think, your AI assistant might already be reading your .env file without you even knowing.
